title: Chapter 4 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Loops, Ranges, and Iteration!

questions.question: In Kotlin, what does the `..` operator create? (e.g., `1..5`)
questions.explanation: The `..` operator easily creates a mathematically closed Range object.

questions.question: If you create a range using `1..10`, is the number 10 included inside?
questions.explanation: Ranges created with `..` are closed, meaning they INCLUDE the ending limit.

questions.question: To create a range that specifically EXCLUDES the final number, use the keyword: 1 _____ 10
questions.explanation: The keyword `until` creates a half-open range, excluding the upper limit.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a `for` loop?
options: To check if a condition is true once.
To execute a block of code repeatedly over sequence of items.
questions.explanation: Loops automate repetitive tasks across bounds or range items.

questions.question: How many times will `for (i in 1..4)` execute its block?
questions.explanation: The range `1..4` contains exactly 4 distinct numbers.

questions.question: In a `for` loop, you want to count backwards from 10 to 1. Which syntax is correct?
questions.explanation: The `downTo` keyword tells Kotlin to step backwards.

questions.question: What keyword allows a `for` loop to skip over numbers? (e.g., counting by 2s or 3s)
questions.explanation: The `step` keyword governs the increment amount between each loop.

questions.question: A `while` loop is specifically designed for scenarios where you already know exactly how many loops you want to perform.
questions.explanation: A `for` loop is entirely better for known ranges! A `while` loop is preferred when the number of loops is UNKNOWN, relying purely on a boolean condition.

questions.question: A `while` loop executes its block continually as long as its condition evaluates to what?
questions.explanation: While true, keep looping. If false, stop branching.

questions.question: What devastating bug happens if the condition inside a `while` loop never evaluates to false?
options: An Infinite Loop occurs, freezing the program forever.
questions.explanation: You must ensure a `while` loop condition eventually forces a false result, otherwise your app hangs entirely.

questions.question: The keyword used to instantly terminate an entire loop and move on is: _________
questions.explanation: `break` is the emergency exit!

questions.question: What does the `continue` keyword do inside a loop?
options: It breaks the cycle and ends the loop.
It skips the remainder of the current loop iteration and instantly jumps to the NEXT loop cycle.
questions.explanation: `continue` skips the rest of the current pass and advances back up to the loop definition header.

questions.question: If you iterate `for (i in 1..5) { if(i==3) continue; print(i) }`, what executes?
questions.explanation: Because `continue` executed when `i` was 3, the `print` beneath it was skipped for that exact cycle.

questions.question: If you iterate `for (i in 1..5) { if(i==3) break; print(i) }`, what executes?
questions.explanation: Because `break` executed when `i` was 3, the entirety of the loop was instantly destroyed. 4 and 5 were never reached.

questions.question: A loop condition `while (10 > 5)` guarantees an infinite loop if left unmodified.
questions.explanation: 10 is always greater than 5. It will never evaluate to false on its own!

questions.question: What is the output of `for (i in 10 downTo 1 step 3) print(i)`?
questions.explanation: It starts at 10, counts backwards, jumping 3 numbers each time: 10, 7, 4, 1.

questions.question: Which loop is generally best when you need to iterate exactly over every item in a List or array?
questions.explanation: A `for` loop is explicitly built for safely iterating through established Sequences or Collections.

questions.question: In the statement `for (item in 1..5)`, what is `item`?
options: A temporary loop variable providing access to the current element.
questions.explanation: `item` is just a temporary, dynamically scoped variable holding the live iteration data.

questions.question: You can place an `if` statement inside a `for` loop.
questions.explanation: You can nest condition statements logic and loops infinitely, granting limitless control.

questions.question: Which of these lines creates a valid loop iterating from 0 to 9?
questions.explanation: `until 10` evaluates specifically to 0 through 9.
